author: thegaram33
content: Previously at Scroll we've had issues of some geth-generated witnesses missing entries required by revm (though we might be using outdated implementations). Is the purpose of the execution witness standardization to eliminate these differences?

author: draganrakita
content: BAL should clarify those cases, and make witnesses canonical. For [example](https://github.com/bluealloy/revm/issues/3484) geth previously included system call, but it got [fixed few](https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/issues/33977) weeks ago. Revm for example had loaded all access list items, this is not a case any more.

author: thegaram33
content: Thanks! In our case the most recent discrepancy was that revm expected to read the delegated code, even if the call failed on the balance check: https://github.com/scroll-tech/go-ethereum/pull/1277/changes

author: kevaundray
content: yep exactly -- there is a canonical execution witness that is "minimal"
